metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A long-legged flightless avian folk city where Strider Wind-Stilt Aerie of the Long Legs, Border Wall Beacon, Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Kassovar Reach should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Kassovar Reach organizes power through Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat, public response through Border Wall Beacon, civic memory through Strider Wind-Stilt Aerie of the Long Legs, and daily life through S1 Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, S2 Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, S5 Arena / Training Civic Hall.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Cedar, Hide, Rammed Earth, Turquoise Inlay, Dyed Wool, Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, Cliffside, Beast Bonding, Ancestor Guidance, Flock Sight, and exact anchors including Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat, Border Wall Beacon, Strider Wind-Stilt Aerie of the Long Legs, Training Arena, Great Forge, Star Observatory, Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, Woven Banners Monument, Watch Bastion Nook, Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A long-legged flightless avian folk civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Strider Wind-Stilt Aerie of the Long Legs, Border Wall Beacon, Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat, and section anchors such as Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall, Civic Services Hall, Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, Arena / Training Civic Hall.

canonLocks: Kassovar Reach uses city code KRH.
The Wonder is Strider Wind-Stilt Aerie of the Long Legs.
The Beacon is Border Wall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.

The source-backed section spine is: S1 Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, S2 Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, S5 Arena / Training Civic Hall.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Kassovar Reach as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Kassovar Reach's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

forms: Strider Wind-Stilt Aerie of the Long Legs
skyline: Strider Wind-Stilt Aerie of the Long Legs
infrastructure: records, permits, markets, services, or rituals implied by anchors
colorLanguage: local textile, stone, metal, vegetation, or water colors from the image package

civilization.people: The population should read as long-legged flightless avian folk and city-specific: citizens occupy streets, shops, public thresholds, and landmark approaches as people who understand the local civic order.
socialClasses: laborers, carriers, messengers, and maintenance crews
households tied to local district obligations
civilization.governance: Government is centered on Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat. Its authority should be visible through courts, records, public access routes, civic lamps, guards or attendants, and the way citizens respond to D13.
civilization.religion: Religious or ritual life should only be emphasized where source anchors support it, especially names such as none explicitly dominant in the current anchors.

bodyLanguage: workers move with practiced familiarity around district anchors

speechPatterns: public notices are tied to section anchors

streets.appearance: Streets should show Kassovar Reach's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, S2 Heavy Industry / Foundry Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Observatory / Astronomy Civic Hall, S5 Arena / Training Civic Hall. Keep roads, plazas, stairs, thresholds, lamps, signs-without-readable-text, props, and civilians aligned to the source-backed materials and landmarks.
streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.
sounds: landmark-specific sounds from water, bells, animals, machines, or crowds when source-backed
smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

common: street services tied to section anchors
exports: specialized tools or ritual goods as supported by anchors
imports: dyes, oils, spices, timber, stone, metal, glass, or cloth as needed by the architecture

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Feather Fan Roofs Wind-Crown Seat, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
